---
type: entity
created: 2026-06-15
updated: 2026-06-15
sources: ["[statement-gdc-wsc_march-2024](../sources/statement-gdc-wsc_march-2024.qmd)"]
tags: [organization]
aliases:
  - "International Electrotechnical Commission"
  - "IEC"
---


# International Electrotechnical Commission

## Basic Information
- Type: organization
- Source: [statement-gdc-wsc_march-2024](../sources/statement-gdc-wsc_march-2024.qmd)

## Description
The International Electrotechnical Commission is a global, not-for-profit membership organization that develops international standards for all electrical, electronic, and related technologies. As a member of the [World Standards Cooperation](world-standards-cooperation.qmd), it plays a critical role in defining technical specifications that ensure the safety, efficiency, and interoperability of electronic systems. It contributes to digital cooperation by providing a consensus-based framework for industry and government stakeholders.

## Related Entities
- [World Standards Cooperation](world-standards-cooperation.qmd)
- [International Organization for Standardization](international-organization-for-standardization.qmd)
- [International Telecommunication Union](international-telecommunication-union.qmd)

## Related Concepts
- [International Standards](../concepts/international-standards.qmd)

## Mentions in Source
- "The world’s leading developers of international standards – the International Electrotechnical Commission, the International Organization for Standardization, and the International Telecommunication Union – provide a trusted platform for digital cooperation among governments, industry, academia, and civil society." — [statement-gdc-wsc_march-2024](../sources/statement-gdc-wsc_march-2024.qmd)
